temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.summary: A measure of the average kinetic energy of randomly moving particles in a substance.
temperature.intuition: The hotter a body, the faster its particles move.

energy.name: Energy
energy.summary: A physical quantity that characterises the ability of a system to do work.
energy.intuition: Energy can change from one form to another but never disappears.

thermal_motion.name: Thermal motion
thermal_motion.summary: The continuous random motion of atoms, molecules, and ions in a substance.
thermal_motion.intuition: Particles of any substance are never at rest — even at low temperatures.

electronic_energy_level.name: Electron energy level
electronic_energy_level.summary: A permitted energy value for an electron in an atom; it can only take discrete (quantised) values.
electronic_energy_level.intuition: An electron in an atom can only occupy 'allowed steps' — like a staircase, not a ramp.

ground_state.name: Ground state
ground_state.summary: The state of an atom with the lowest possible total electron energy. Electrons fill the lowest available orbitals according to the Aufbau principle.
ground_state.intuition: A ball resting at the bottom of a pit. Energy must be added to raise it higher.

excited_state.name: Excited state
excited_state.summary: A temporary state of an atom in which one or more electrons occupy a higher energy level than in the ground state. Unstable — the atom quickly returns to the ground state.
excited_state.intuition: A ball pushed up a slope will roll back down. An excited atom likewise quickly returns to the ground state, emitting a photon.

photon.name: Photon
photon.summary: A quantum of electromagnetic radiation. A photon's energy E = hν is proportional to its frequency ν. Each colour of visible light corresponds to a precisely defined photon energy.
photon.intuition: A photon is a 'packet' of light. Blue light carries more energetic photons than red light.

collision.name: Particle collision
collision.summary: An encounter between two particles (molecules, atoms, ions) close enough to interact. The collision rate depends on temperature and concentration.
collision.intuition: The more balls on a billiard table and the faster they move, the more often they collide.

effective_collision.name: Effective collision
effective_collision.summary: A collision in which the energy of the encounter exceeds the activation energy of the reaction. Only such collisions lead to a chemical reaction.
effective_collision.intuition: Not every hammer blow drives a nail — you need a sufficiently strong strike. Similarly, only sufficiently energetic collisions lead to a reaction.
